integrity_note: This file is a versioned point of truth. Any modification after frozen_date invalidates the corpus and requires a new version bump.
corpus_version_policy: Semantic versioning. PATCH for metadata fixes (typos, notes). MINOR for adding new pilots. MAJOR for reclassifying existing pilots or changing level criteria.

version_history.change: Initial frozen corpus: 4 ACCEPT (Level B), 3 INDETERMINATE (Level C)

Full ORI-C pipeline on 3 densified Level C pilots. Pantheon SN reclassified INDETERMINATE -> ACCEPT (Level B). PBDB marine and LLM scaling reclassified INDETERMINATE -> REJECT (Level B). All 7 pilots now decidable.

A.label: Canonical
A.scope: Core validation (synthetic + FRED + protocol + dual proof)
requirements: Full statistical support with confusion matrix

B.label: Conclusive real pilots
B.scope: Out-of-core real datasets with exploitable verdict

C.label: Exploratory under precheck
C.scope: Signal or plausibility present, canonical proof level not reached
requirements: At least one blocking constraint identified

pilots.signal_description: EEG seizure detection with 5 class labels across 500 segments
Solar cycle regime with sunspot number thresholds
COVID excess mortality threshold across 4 countries (FR, IT, US, BE)

pilots.upgrade_note: Upgraded from C (INDETERMINATE, 100 pts) to B (ACCEPT, 150 pts) after full pipeline on densified data

pilots.signal_description: BTC price regime transition with volatility threshold
Marine biodiversity mass extinction threshold (>400 Myr)
pilots.upgrade_note: Upgraded from C (INDETERMINATE, 100 pts) to B (REJECT, 140 pts). C1 failed: no transition detected (det_rate=0.0). This is a true negative — the ORI-C framework does not detect a cumulative symbolic threshold in marine diversity data.
Upgraded from C (INDETERMINATE, 60 pts) to B (REJECT, 120 pts). C1 failed: no transition detected (det_rate=0.0, sigma_zero_post=1.0). Original data was synthetic-calibrated, not real benchmark data.

reading_note: All 7 pilots are now at Level B with decidable verdicts. 5 ACCEPT across 5 domains (Finance, Health, Neurosciences, Astrophysique, Cosmologie). 2 REJECT (Paleobiologie, IA/Technologie). The framework discriminates: it does not detect a transition in every dataset. The 2 REJECT verdicts are scientifically valuable — they demonstrate that ORI-C has specificity, not just sensitivity.
